user.title: Add Tuya E-Board
user.description: Power your board on (turn the remote on so it's awake) and bring it within Bluetooth range of Home Assistant, then start the scan. The board sleeps when idle, so it's only discoverable while it's on.

no_bluetooth.title: No Bluetooth adapter
no_bluetooth.description: Home Assistant has no Bluetooth adapter available, so it can't scan. Connect a Bluetooth adapter, run Home Assistant on hardware that has one, or add an ESPHome Bluetooth Proxy — then try again.

no_devices.title: No board found
no_devices.description: Bluetooth is working, but no Tuya e-board was advertising. Make sure the board is powered on (remote awake) and in Bluetooth range, then scan again.

pick.title: Select board
pick.description: Choose the discovered board to add.

credentials.title: Credentials for {name}
credentials.description: Enter the Tuya local key for this board (from the Tuya IoT cloud via `tinytuya wizard`). The values are verified by a one-time connect and read.

data_description.local_key: The device's local key from devices.json / the Tuya IoT cloud. Re-pairing the board rotates it.
data_description.product_id: Selects the per-product DP map (units/scales). Leave blank to use defaults.

error.cannot_connect: Couldn't reach the board. Make sure it's powered on with the remote awake and in Bluetooth range, then try again.
error.invalid_auth: Connected, but the handshake failed — the local key is likely wrong or stale (re-pairing the board rotates it). Re-run `tinytuya wizard` and re-enter it.

abort.no_devices_found: No Tuya e-board found advertising. Power the board on (remote awake) and in range, then retry.
